title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: wpgdata provides tidy R interface City Winnipeg Open Data Portal. Discover available datasets, inspect schemas, download records automatic parallel pagination — via Socrata OData V4 Discovery APIs. vignette walks four core functions using Assessment Parcels dataset (d4mq-wa44) primary working example, additional examples using 311 Service Requests dataset (u7f6-5326) demonstrate date filtering large datasets.
code: library(wpgdata) library(dplyr)

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: typical wpgdata workflow follows four steps:
code: peg_catalogue()   →   peg_info()      →   peg_metadata()   →   peg_data() (find datasets)       (dataset info)      (column schema)      (fetch rows)

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: peg_catalogue() retrieves every published dataset portal. catalogue pages per-dataset metadata fetched parallel, full catalogue arrives quickly regardless size. Count datasets category get overview ’s available: Search name find dataset retrieve ID: Use id value peg_* function: Use limit cap results exploring:
code: catalogue <- peg_catalogue() catalogue #> # A tibble: 216 × 22 #>    id        name     description category license_id created_at rows_updated_at #>    <chr>     <chr>    <chr>       <chr>    <chr>      <date>     <date>          #>  1 d4mq-wa44 Assessm… "List of a… Assessm… OGL_CANADA 2017-08-23 2026-03-10      #>  2 yg42-q284 WFPS Ca… "The data … Fire an… OGL_CANADA 2020-12-14 2026-03-10      #>  3 iibp-28fx Burial … "Locations… Cemeter… OGL_CANADA 2016-01-29 2026-03-10      #>  4 vrzk-mj7v 311 Cal… "Caller wa… Contact… OGL_CANADA 2022-06-17 2026-03-10      #>  5 gnxp-9hpt Public … "Public No… Develop… NA         2016-08-08 2026-03-10      #>  6 tix9-r5tc Plow Zo… "Scheduled… City Pl… NA         2016-10-18 2026-03-10      #>  7 du7c-8488 Daily A… "The data … Insect … NA         2016-05-04 2026-03-10      #>  8 pfbi-rm6v FIPPA R… "The Freed… Organiz… OGL_CANADA 2019-09-10 2026-03-10      #>  9 tgrf-v2zc River W… "Record of… Water a… OGL_CANADA 2018-03-15 2026-03-10      #> 10 qe3f-4r3j Active … "The City … Neighbo… OGL_CANADA 2023-06-30 2026-03-10      #> # ℹ 206 more rows #> # ℹ 15 more variables: view_last_modified <date>, publication_date <date>, #> #   index_updated_at <date>, row_count <int>, col_count <int>, #> #   download_count <int>, view_count <int>, group <chr>, department <chr>, #> #   update_frequency <chr>, quality_rank <chr>, license <chr>, #> #   license_link <chr>, tags <list>, url <chr> catalogue |>   count(category, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 26 × 2 #>    category                                                   n #>    <chr>                                                  <int> #>  1 Census                                                    35 #>  2 City Planning                                             27 #>  3 Development Approvals, Building Permits, & Inspections    23 #>  4 Transportation Planning & Traffic Management              18 #>  5 Uncategorized                                             16 #>  6 Council Services                                          15 #>  7 Recreation                                                 9 #>  8 Organizational Support Services                            8 #>  9 Assessment, Taxation, & Corporate                          7 #> 10 Contact Centre - 311                                       7 #> # ℹ 16 more rows catalogue |>   filter(grepl("assessment", name, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>   select(name, id, rows_updated_at) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   name               id        rows_updated_at #>   <chr>              <chr>     <date>          #> 1 Assessment Parcels d4mq-wa44 2026-03-10 dataset_id <- catalogue |>   filter(name == "Assessment Parcels") |>   pull(id)  dataset_id #> [1] "d4mq-wa44" peg_catalogue(limit = 10) #> # A tibble: 10 × 22 #>    id        name     description category license_id created_at rows_updated_at #>    <chr>     <chr>    <chr>       <chr>    <chr>      <date>     <date>          #>  1 d4mq-wa44 Assessm… "List of a… Assessm… OGL_CANADA 2017-08-23 2026-03-10      #>  2 yg42-q284 WFPS Ca… "The data … Fire an… OGL_CANADA 2020-12-14 2026-03-10      #>  3 iibp-28fx Burial … "Locations… Cemeter… OGL_CANADA 2016-01-29 2026-03-10      #>  4 vrzk-mj7v 311 Cal… "Caller wa… Contact… OGL_CANADA 2022-06-17 2026-03-10      #>  5 gnxp-9hpt Public … "Public No… Develop… NA         2016-08-08 2026-03-10      #>  6 hfwk-jp4h Tree In… "Detailed … Parks    OGL_CANADA 2017-08-22 2026-03-09      #>  7 p5sy-gt7y Aggrega… "Aggregate… Develop… NA         2016-12-21 2026-03-09      #>  8 it4w-cpf4 Detaile… "City of W… Develop… NA         2016-04-18 2026-03-01      #>  9 4her-3th5 311 Ser… "This data… Contact… NA         2015-07-22 2025-04-15      #> 10 6rcy-9uik NA        NA         Uncateg… NA         NA         NA              #> # ℹ 15 more variables: view_last_modified <date>, publication_date <date>, #> #   index_updated_at <date>, row_count <int>, col_count <int>, #> #   download_count <int>, view_count <int>, group <chr>, department <chr>, #> #   update_frequency <chr>, quality_rank <chr>, license <chr>, #> #   license_link <chr>, tags <list>, url <chr>

what: Step 2 — Explore a dataset with peg_info()
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: peg_info() returns high-level metadata single dataset — name, description, category, update frequency, row count, license: useful committing large download: tells data last updated, many rows expect, license published .
code: peg_info("d4mq-wa44") #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>   name        description category created_at rows_updated_at view_last_modified #>   <chr>       <chr>       <chr>    <date>     <date>          <date>             #> 1 Assessment… List of al… Assessm… 2017-08-23 2026-03-10      2026-03-10         #> # ℹ 5 more variables: view_count <int>, download_count <int>, tags <list>, #> #   license <chr>, provenance <chr>

what: Step 3 — Find field names with peg_metadata()
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: OData queries require exact field names. Use peg_metadata() look available fields types writing query: field_name column contains names use peg_data(). type column tells whether field text, number, floating timestamp, another type — important writing correct filter expressions. Find numeric fields : Find timestamp fields (relevant date filtering):
code: meta <- peg_metadata("d4mq-wa44") meta #> # A tibble: 71 × 4 #>    name               field_name         type   description #>    <chr>              <chr>              <chr>  <chr>       #>  1 Roll Number        roll_number        text   NA          #>  2 Street Number      street_number      number NA          #>  3 Unit Number        unit_number        text   NA          #>  4 Street Suffix      street_suffix      text   NA          #>  5 Street Direction   street_direction   text   NA          #>  6 Street Name        street_name        text   NA          #>  7 Street Type        street_type        text   NA          #>  8 Full Address       full_address       text   NA          #>  9 Neighbourhood Area neighbourhood_area text   NA          #> 10 Market Region      market_region      text   NA          #> # ℹ 61 more rows meta |>   filter(type == "number") #> # A tibble: 24 × 4 #>    name                            field_name                  type  description #>    <chr>                           <chr>                       <chr> <chr>       #>  1 Street Number                   street_number               numb… NA          #>  2 Total Living Area               total_living_area           numb… In Square … #>  3 Year Built                      year_built                  numb… NA          #>  4 Rooms                           rooms                       numb… NA          #>  5 Number Floors (Condo)           number_floors_condo         numb… NA          #>  6 Assessed Land Area              assessed_land_area          numb… In Square … #>  7 Water Frontage Measurement      water_frontage_measurement  numb… in Feet     #>  8 Sewer Frontage Measurement      sewer_frontage_measurement  numb… In Feet     #>  9 Total Assessed Value            total_assessed_value        numb… NA          #> 10 Total Proposed Assessment Value total_proposed_assessment_… numb… NA          #> # ℹ 14 more rows meta |>   filter(type == "calendar_date") #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   name                     field_name               type          description #>   <chr>                    <chr>                    <chr>         <chr>       #> 1 Assessment Date          assessment_date          calendar_date ""          #> 2 Proposed Assessment Date proposed_assessment_date calendar_date NA

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: peg_data() single function fetching rows. supports server-side filtering, column selection, sorting, offset pagination. pages fetched parallel automatically — manual pagination needed.

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Fetch small sample inspect structure larger query:
code: peg_data("d4mq-wa44", top = 5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 72 #>   `__id`    roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>   <chr>     <chr>               <int> <chr>       <lgl>         <lgl>            #> 1 row-ab5p… 01000001000          1636 NA          NA            NA               #> 2 row-xxte… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #> 3 row-cmgt… 01000008000          1574 NA          NA            NA               #> 4 row-v9ih… 01000008200          1550 NA          NA            NA               #> 5 row-t6sd… 01000008400          1538 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <int>, #> #   property_use_code <chr>, assessed_land_area <int>, …

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Pass R expressions directly — wpgdata translates OData syntax: use raw OData strings prefer: approaches produce identical results.
code: peg_data("d4mq-wa44",   filter = total_assessed_value > 1000000,   top    = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <chr>       <chr>         <chr>            #>  1 row-xxt… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #>  2 row-pq8… 01000013200          1520 NA          NA            NA               #>  3 row-pbe… 01000014500          1450 NA          NA            NA               #>  4 row-jnp… 01000045500          1290 NA          NA            NA               #>  5 row-xmc… 01000064000          1820 NA          NA            NA               #>  6 row-p55… 01000067500          1916 NA          NA            NA               #>  7 row-52k… 01000067900          1892 NA          NA            NA               #>  8 row-tap… 01000092200          1700 NA          NA            NA               #>  9 row-mdd… 01000096000          1720 NA          NA            NA               #> 10 row-epn… 01000306500          2424 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <lgl>, #> #   property_use_code <chr>, assessed_land_area <int>, … peg_data("d4mq-wa44",   filter = "total_assessed_value gt 1000000",   top    = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <chr>       <chr>         <chr>            #>  1 row-xxt… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #>  2 row-pq8… 01000013200          1520 NA          NA            NA               #>  3 row-pbe… 01000014500          1450 NA          NA            NA               #>  4 row-jnp… 01000045500          1290 NA          NA            NA               #>  5 row-xmc… 01000064000          1820 NA          NA            NA               #>  6 row-p55… 01000067500          1916 NA          NA            NA               #>  7 row-52k… 01000067900          1892 NA          NA            NA               #>  8 row-tap… 01000092200          1700 NA          NA            NA               #>  9 row-mdd… 01000096000          1720 NA          NA            NA               #> 10 row-epn… 01000306500          2424 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <lgl>, #> #   property_use_code <chr>, assessed_land_area <int>, …

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Combine conditions & () | ():
code: peg_data("d4mq-wa44",   filter = total_assessed_value > 1000000 & building_type == "TWO STOREY",   top    = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 72 #>    `__id`   roll_number street_number unit_number street_suffix street_direction #>    <chr>    <chr>               <int> <chr>       <lgl>         <chr>            #>  1 row-xxt… 01000005500          1584 NA          NA            NA               #>  2 row-pq8… 01000013200          1520 NA          NA            NA               #>  3 row-pbe… 01000014500          1450 NA          NA            NA               #>  4 row-xmc… 01000064000          1820 NA          NA            NA               #>  5 row-55u… 01000560000          3179 NA          NA            NA               #>  6 row-mqy… 01000615000             3 NA          NA            NA               #>  7 row-3w2… 01000615400            17 NA          NA            NA               #>  8 row-k94… 01000615800            31 NA          NA            NA               #>  9 row-ttt… 01000617400            36 NA          NA            NA               #> 10 row-97f… 01000718800           400 NA          NA            NA               #> # ℹ 66 more variables: street_name <chr>, street_type <chr>, #> #   full_address <chr>, neighbourhood_area <chr>, market_region <chr>, #> #   total_living_area <int>, building_type <chr>, basement <chr>, #> #   basement_finish <chr>, year_built <int>, rooms <int>, #> #   air_conditioning <chr>, fire_place <chr>, attached_garage <chr>, #> #   detached_garage <chr>, pool <chr>, number_floors_condo <lgl>, #> #   property_use_code <chr>, assessed_land_area <int>, …

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Use select return columns need — reduces transfer size significantly wide datasets:
code: peg_data("d4mq-wa44",   select = c("roll_number", "full_address", "total_assessed_value",              "building_type", "year_built"),   top    = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 5 #>    roll_number full_address       building_type  year_built total_assessed_value #>    <chr>       <chr>              <chr>               <int>                <int> #>  1 01000001000 1636 MCCREARY ROAD ONE STOREY           1991               723000 #>  2 01000005500 1584 MCCREARY ROAD TWO STOREY           1991              1619000 #>  3 01000008000 1574 MCCREARY ROAD ONE STOREY           2007               570000 #>  4 01000008200 1550 MCCREARY ROAD ONE STOREY           1982               743000 #>  5 01000008400 1538 MCCREARY ROAD ONE STOREY           1970               577000 #>  6 01000008500 1536 MCCREARY ROAD 4 LEVEL SPLIT        1958               979000 #>  7 01000013200 1520 MCCREARY ROAD TWO STOREY           2021              1900000 #>  8 01000013300 1510 MCCREARY ROAD ONE & 1/2 STO…       2000               995000 #>  9 01000013600 1500 MCCREARY ROAD ONE & 1/2 STO…       1994               669000 #> 10 01000013700 1490 MCCREARY ROAD CABOVER              2008               882000

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Use orderby sort ascending descending:
code: peg_data("d4mq-wa44",   select  = c("roll_number", "full_address", "total_assessed_value"),   orderby = "total_assessed_value desc",   top     = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #>    roll_number full_address                total_assessed_value #>    <chr>       <chr>                                      <int> #>  1 13099071230 1485 PORTAGE AVENUE                    651316000 #>  2 03091643600 92 DYSART ROAD                         475244000 #>  3 08020955700 1225 ST MARY'S ROAD                    328848000 #>  4 13096152000 700 WILLIAM AVENUE                     262782000 #>  5 12092819100 10 KENNEDY STREET                      262044000 #>  6 12093468100 242 HARGRAVE STREET                    214972000 #>  7 06072082500 409 TACHE AVENUE                       206873000 #>  8 10006776045 555 STERLING LYON PARKWAY              200244000 #>  9 07055050000 T-35-2000 WELLINGTON AVENUE            180099000 #> 10 09010473150 1555 REGENT AVENUE W                   162335000

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
code: peg_data("d4mq-wa44",   filter  = total_assessed_value > 1000000 & year_built > 2000,   select  = c("roll_number", "full_address", "total_assessed_value",               "building_type", "year_built"),   orderby = "total_assessed_value desc",   top     = 10 ) #> # A tibble: 10 × 5 #>    roll_number full_address        building_type year_built total_assessed_value #>    <chr>       <chr>               <chr>              <int>                <int> #>  1 10004062400 23 KERSLAKE PLACE   TWO STOREY          2006              4235000 #>  2 10003003000 137 HANDSART BOULE… TWO STOREY          2006              4077000 #>  3 10002999100 214 GRENFELL BOULE… TWO STOREY          2009              4054000 #>  4 10006777080 36 AVONLYNN COURT   ONE STOREY          2022              3905000 #>  5 12040844000 885 WELLINGTON CRE… TWO STOREY          2021              3855000 #>  6 10002760000 135 PARK BOULEVARD… TWO STOREY          2023              3643000 #>  7 01002770500 70 RIDGEDALE CRESC… TWO STOREY          2024              3486000 #>  8 10002884000 130 HANDSART BOULE… TWO STOREY          2020              3386000 #>  9 10006776560 124 GRENFELL BOULE… TWO STOREY          2017              3335000 #> 10 10003006000 123 HANDSART BOULE… TWO STOREY          2005              3304000

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Use skip top together retrieve specific slice rows:
code: # rows 1–5 page_1 <- peg_data("d4mq-wa44", top = 5, skip = 0)  # rows 6–10 page_2 <- peg_data("d4mq-wa44", top = 5, skip = 5)

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Omit top fetch every matching row across pages:
code: # fetches all rows — may take several minutes for large datasets peg_data("d4mq-wa44")

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Datasets timestamp columns (type calendar_date floating_timestamp peg_metadata()) support date range filters via raw OData strings. Socrata floating timestamps use ISO 8601 format milliseconds: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.mmm. Build cutoff strings small helper avoid repeating format: examples use 311 Service Requests dataset (u7f6-5326), 18+ million rows updated continuously — date filters essential avoid downloading entire dataset.
code: # helper — format a Date as a Socrata floating timestamp string ts <- function(date) format(date, "%Y-%m-%dT00:00:00.000")

what: Records closed in the last 4 days
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
code: filter_str <- paste0("closed_date ge '", ts(Sys.Date() - 4), "'")  peg_data(   "u7f6-5326",   filter = filter_str,   select = c("case_id", "channel_type", "subject", "reason",              "type", "open_date", "closed_date", "case_status",              "neighbourhood", "ward"),   top    = 100L ) #> # A tibble: 100 × 10 #>    case_id   channel_type subject reason type  open_date closed_date case_status #>    <chr>     <chr>        <chr>   <chr>  <chr> <chr>     <chr>       <chr>       #>  1 925ba982… Voice In     Servic… Publi… Pave… 2018-04-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  2 d81f9c30… VOF          Servic… Publi… Graf… 2023-12-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  3 5b52f3b4… VOF          Servic… Publi… Graf… 2023-12-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  4 71c5dea2… VOF          Servic… Publi… Graf… 2023-12-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  5 c48b7cd1… Dept Create… Servic… Publi… Graf… 2023-12-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  6 b68d46fc… VOF          Servic… Publi… Graf… 2024-01-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  7 9c5748e5… e-mail In    Servic… Publi… Graf… 2024-01-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  8 b3e723ff… e-mail In    Servic… Publi… Graf… 2024-02-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  9 15271674… e-mail In    Servic… Publi… Graf… 2024-03-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #> 10 d19dadf0… VOF          Servic… Publi… Graf… 2024-03-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #> # ℹ 90 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: neighbourhood <chr>, ward <chr>

what: Records closed within a specific date range
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
code: filter_str <- paste0(   "closed_date ge '", ts(as.Date("2026-03-01")), "'",   " and ",   "closed_date lt '", ts(as.Date("2026-03-07")), "'" )  peg_data(   "u7f6-5326",   filter = filter_str,   select = c("case_id", "subject", "open_date", "closed_date", "case_status"),   top    = 100L ) #> # A tibble: 100 × 5 #>    case_id                             subject open_date closed_date case_status #>    <chr>                               <chr>   <chr>     <chr>       <chr>       #>  1 925ba9824f63b6e105eb00dc8f069b56e4… Servic… 2018-04-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  2 ef9eb1f31d08f9c23314e28c86c4cdc66a… Servic… 2023-02-… 2026-03-04… Closed      #>  3 e17ba4e93b31b085f5f102113833ecc3b8… Servic… 2023-06-… 2026-03-05… Closed      #>  4 e17ba4e93b31b085f5f102113833ecc3b8… Servic… 2023-06-… 2026-03-05… Closed      #>  5 c54dfa99aa65b99836b03401b63f7576f8… Servic… 2023-08-… 2026-03-04… Closed      #>  6 b6bd446227ce1aef7480f1aec1e60d97cc… Servic… 2023-09-… 2026-03-04… Closed      #>  7 d81f9c3026c55e7b9fd049c13cb825f03f… Servic… 2023-12-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  8 5b52f3b44ddc0fdaffe18f93c7529579b9… Servic… 2023-12-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #>  9 71c5dea21dbeba15537e1b26084bc6b908… Servic… 2023-12-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #> 10 c48b7cd13abf00d7de74c92cf918efc15b… Servic… 2023-12-… 2026-03-06… Closed      #> # ℹ 90 more rows

what: Open cases from the last 7 days
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
code: filter_str <- paste0(   "open_date ge '", ts(Sys.Date() - 7), "'",   " and ",   "case_status eq 'Open'" )  peg_data(   "u7f6-5326",   filter  = filter_str,   select  = c("case_id", "subject", "reason", "open_date",               "case_status", "neighbourhood", "ward"),   top     = 100L ) #> # A tibble: 100 × 7 #>    case_id              subject reason open_date case_status neighbourhood ward  #>    <chr>                <chr>   <chr>  <chr>     <chr>       <chr>         <chr> #>  1 651af1c08e9e943d4c6… Servic… Water… 2026-03-… Open        Garden City   Old … #>  2 0b8f964f48a39e42332… Servic… Water… 2026-03-… Open        NA            NA    #>  3 ffa4c42ea341c4b0a82… Servic… Water… 2026-03-… Open        West Alexand… Poin… #>  4 cdc7299136ca05917c7… Servic… Publi… 2026-03-… Open        Assiniboia D… Char… #>  5 738c5f21c1420e128ee… Servic… Publi… 2026-03-… Open        NA            NA    #>  6 16c8bd44b69b98665cc… Servic… Water… 2026-03-… Open        NA            NA    #>  7 87d4bb9f18e575fc7a6… Servic… Water… 2026-03-… Open        NA            NA    #>  8 71a97c142fecf839d6f… Servic… Publi… 2026-03-… Open        NA            NA    #>  9 bb1a3cc58a09a7a1044… Servic… Publi… 2026-03-… Open        Wellington C… Rive… #> 10 fd3917710d58a0fe5e5… Servic… Publi… 2026-03-… Open        Weston        Poin… #> # ℹ 90 more rows

what: Cases closed yesterday, sorted by most recently closed
title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Note: top argument included date filter examples keep live API calls lightweight development. Remove retrieve full result set.
code: filter_str <- paste0(   "closed_date ge '", ts(Sys.Date() - 1), "'",   " and ",   "closed_date lt '", ts(Sys.Date()), "'" )  peg_data(   "u7f6-5326",   filter  = filter_str,   select  = c("case_id", "subject", "channel_type",               "open_date", "closed_date", "neighbourhood"),   orderby = "closed_date desc",   top     = 100L ) #> # A tibble: 8 × 6 #>   case_id               channel_type subject open_date closed_date neighbourhood #>   <chr>                 <chr>        <chr>   <chr>     <chr>       <chr>         #> 1 e7aeea7ab60a8ea2a38d… Voice In     Servic… 2026-03-… 2026-03-09… Wildwood      #> 2 3196e0629b27fd06c63a… Voice In     Inform… 2026-03-… 2026-03-09… NA            #> 3 9ceb51f74afd7cf65cf6… Voice In     Inform… 2026-03-… 2026-03-09… NA            #> 4 ec2beb625c867971ebad… Voice In     Inform… 2026-03-… 2026-03-09… NA            #> 5 d16150e9e7fc38d82621… e-mail In    Servic… 2026-03-… 2026-03-09… NA            #> 6 becae90412837deb7a7f… Voice In     Inform… 2026-03-… 2026-03-09… NA            #> 7 fa7630c2d7b2cb195025… Voice In     Inform… 2026-03-… 2026-03-09… NA            #> 8 1dee42a9a3f437aba836… Voice In     Inform… 2026-03-… 2026-03-09… NA

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: Tip: R expression syntax works numeric string comparisons. date comparisons, use raw OData strings shown date filtering section .

title: Getting Started with wpgdata
text: easiest way search directly R: Alternatively, browse City Winnipeg Open Data Portal, open dataset, click API → OData V4, copy last segment URL:
code: peg_catalogue() |>   filter(grepl("your search term", name, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>   select(name, id, category) https://data.winnipeg.ca/api/odata/v4/d4mq-wa44                                       ^^^^^^^^^^                                       dataset ID

what: wpgdata — Winnipeg Open Data in R
title: wpgdata — Winnipeg Open Data in R
text: wpgdata provides tidy R interface City Winnipeg Open Data Portal. Discover available datasets, inspect schemas, download records automatic parallel pagination — via Socrata OData V4 Discovery APIs.

title: wpgdata — Winnipeg Open Data in R
text: Install CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("wpgdata") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("myominnoo/wpgdata")

title: wpgdata — Winnipeg Open Data in R
text: package exposes four functions follow natural progression discovery download:
code: library(wpgdata) peg_catalogue()   →   peg_info()      →   peg_metadata()   →   peg_data() (find datasets)       (dataset info)      (column schema)      (fetch rows)

title: wpgdata — Winnipeg Open Data in R
text: List every dataset published Winnipeg Open Data Portal. catalogue pages per-dataset metadata fetched parallel, full catalogue arrives seconds rather minutes. Use dplyr explore catalogue: Use limit cap number datasets returned exploring:
code: peg_catalogue() #> # A tibble: 216 × 22 #>    id        name     description category license_id created_at rows_updated_at #>    <chr>     <chr>    <chr>       <chr>    <chr>      <date>     <date>          #>  1 d4mq-wa44 Assessm… "List of a… Assessm… OGL_CANADA 2017-08-23 2026-03-10      #>  2 yg42-q284 WFPS Ca… "The data … Fire an… OGL_CANADA 2020-12-14 2026-03-10      #>  3 iibp-28fx Burial … "Locations… Cemeter… OGL_CANADA 2016-01-29 2026-03-10      #>  4 vrzk-mj7v 311 Cal… "Caller wa… Contact… OGL_CANADA 2022-06-17 2026-03-10      #>  5 gnxp-9hpt Public … "Public No… Develop… <NA>       2016-08-08 2026-03-10      #>  6 tix9-r5tc Plow Zo… "Scheduled… City Pl… <NA>       2016-10-18 2026-03-10      #>  7 du7c-8488 Daily A… "The data … Insect … <NA>       2016-05-04 2026-03-10      #>  8 pfbi-rm6v FIPPA R… "The Freed… Organiz… OGL_CANADA 2019-09-10 2026-03-10      #>  9 tgrf-v2zc River W… "Record of… Water a… OGL_CANADA 2018-03-15 2026-03-10      #> 10 qe3f-4r3j Active … "The City … Neighbo… OGL_CANADA 2023-06-30 2026-03-10      #> # code: peg_catalogue() |>   filter(grepl("your search term", name, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>   select(name, id, category) https://data.winnipeg.ca/d/d4mq-wa44                             ^^^^^^^^^^                             dataset ID

title: wpgdata — Winnipeg Open Data in R
text: peg_data() accepts plain R expressions filter argument translates OData automatically. Raw OData strings also accepted advanced use.

title: wpgdata — Winnipeg Open Data in R
text: MIT © Myo Minn Oo

what: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
text: Retrieves catalogue publicly available datasets data.winnipeg.ca using Socrata Discovery API. catalogue pages per-dataset metadata fetched parallel maximum throughput.

title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
code: peg_catalogue(limit = NULL, max_connections = NULL)

title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
text: limit positive integer — maximum number datasets return. NULL (default) every available dataset fetched. max_connections positive integer controlling many HTTP requests -flight . NULL (default), value auto-detected 2 * parallel::detectCores(), capped .MAX_CONNECTIONS (20) number requests needed. Supply explicit value override — lower portal rate-limits, higher headroom.

title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
text: tibble one row per dataset, arranged recently updated.

title: List all datasets from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_catalogue
text: works: Fires catalogue count request first catalogue page simultaneously save one round trip. Pre-computes remaining page URLs known offsets fetches parallel (sequential nextLink chaining). Extracts dataset IDs pages, fires metadata requests single parallel batch via /api/views endpoint.

what: Fetch data from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_data
title: Fetch data from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_data
text: unified replacement peg_get(), peg_query(), peg_all(). Pages fetched parallel maximum throughput.

title: Fetch data from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_data
code: peg_data(   dataset_id,   filter = NULL,   select = NULL,   top = NULL,   skip = NULL,   orderby = NULL,   max_connections = NULL )

title: Fetch data from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_data
text: dataset_id character string dataset ID e.g. "d4mq-wa44". filter filter expression — either R expression total_assessed_value > 500000, raw OData string "total_assessed_value gt 500000". Use peg_metadata() look valid field names. select character vector field names return, e.g. c("roll_number", "total_assessed_value"). top positive integer — maximum number rows return. NULL (default) every row fetched. skip non-negative integer — rows skip collecting results. orderby character string specifying sort order, e.g. "total_assessed_value desc". max_connections positive integer controlling many HTTP requests -flight . NULL (default), value auto-detected 2 * parallel::detectCores(), capped .MAX_CONNECTIONS (20) number pages required. Supply explicit value override — lower server rate-limits, higher headroom.

title: Fetch data from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_data
text: tibble::tibble() requested rows original order.

title: Fetch data from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_data
text: works: Fires $count request first data page simultaneously, saving one full round trip main fetch begins. Pre-computes every remaining page URL $skip offsets — nextLink chaining required. Fires remaining requests via httr2::req_perform_parallel() max_active throttling. curl starts next request instant slot frees, avoiding convoy delay manual batching. Binds pages original order trims top.

title: Fetch data from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # fetch every row peg_data("d4mq-wa44")  # fetch the first 500 rows peg_data("d4mq-wa44", top = 500)  # filter + select + sort, fetch all matching rows peg_data(   "d4mq-wa44",   filter  = total_assessed_value > 500000,   select  = c("roll_number", "total_assessed_value"),   orderby = "total_assessed_value desc" )  # override connection count peg_data("d4mq-wa44", max_connections = 4L) } # }

text: tibble one row containing dataset-level metadata including name, description, category, timestamps, counts, tags, license, provenance.

title: Get dataset-level information from the Winnipeg Open Data Portal — peg_info

title: wpgdata 0.3.0 (development version)
text: peg_get("id") → peg_data("id") peg_query("id", filter = x > 1, select = "col") → peg_data("id", filter = x > 1, select = "col") peg_all("id") → peg_data("id")

title: wpgdata 0.3.0 (development version)
text: peg_data() — replaces peg_get(), peg_query(), peg_all() unified interface fetching dataset rows. Supports server-side filtering, column selection, row ordering, offset pagination via top skip. pages fetched parallel using httr2::req_perform_parallel(), delivering significant throughput improvements previous sequential approach.

title: wpgdata 0.3.0 (development version)
text: Catalogue pages per-dataset metadata now fetched parallel using httr2::req_perform_parallel(), count request first page fired simultaneously save full round trip. Metadata now sourced /api/views/{id}.json endpoint per dataset, replacing Socrata Discovery API sole data source. Added new columns: row_count, col_count, view_count, group, department, update_frequency, quality_rank, license, license_link, tags, license_id, rows_updated_at, view_last_modified, publication_date, index_updated_at. row_count now sourced cached column statistics (cachedContents), making fast without requiring separate OData count request per dataset. Removed default limit 200 — available datasets fetched default. Results sorted rows_updated_at descending. HTTP errors network failures individual datasets caught skipped warning rather aborting entire fetch.

title: wpgdata 0.3.0 (development version)
text: Added input validation dataset_id (rejects NULL, NA, empty string, non-character, length > 1). Added httr2::req_timeout() httr2::req_retry() exponential backoff transient 429, 500, 503 responses. Fixed double-read response body; empty-body check now handled single tryCatch around resp_body_json().

title: wpgdata 0.3.0 (development version)
text: Added input validation dataset_id matching peg_info(). Added httr2::req_timeout() httr2::req_retry() exponential backoff transient 429, 500, 503 responses. Fixed double-read response body. Added %||% NA_character_ fallbacks name, field_name, type — previously silently insert NULL malformed column entries. Replaced soft-deprecated purrr::map_dfr() purrr::map() + purrr::list_rbind().

title: wpgdata 0.2.0
text: peg_catalogue() — list datasets available City Winnipeg Open Data Portal name, ID, category, last updated date.

title: wpgdata 0.2.0
text: Added get_catalogue_count(), fetch_catalogue_page(), parse_catalogue() internal helpers utils.R. Imported rlang::.data pronoun suppress R CMD check notes dplyr::mutate() calls.

title: wpgdata 0.1.0
text: peg_get() — fetch first page dataset. peg_query() — filter, select, sort, paginate datasets. peg_all() — download rows automatic pagination. peg_metadata() — retrieve column names types. peg_info() — retrieve dataset-level information. Supports R expressions raw OData strings peg_query(). Automatic pagination detection progress reporting peg_all().
